articles.title: Cloud cover for 2 days in Rajasthan: Holi week may see intense heat; mercury likely to touch 38°C - Bhaskar English

- Early Summer Likely In Rajasthan As Western Disturbances Weaken, Heatwave like Conditions Possible During Holi | Jaipur May See Temperatures Nearing 38°C In Early March; Met Office Rules Out Significant Rainfall Despite Brief Cloud Cover
Weather swings again in Rajasthan:Light rain in Jaipur, Tonk, early summer heat likely during Holi
- Copy link
Rajasthan witnessed another sudden change in weather after five days, with intermittent rain in Jaipur and Tonk on Monday morning bringing temporary relief from rising temperatures.
Light showers, accompanied by strong winds, led to a drop in temperature and increased chill in parts of Jaipur, Dausa and nearby districts. Several areas, including Malviya Nagar and Sikar Road in Jaipur, reported drizzle from around 7 am, while Chomu recorded heavy rain for nearly 15 minutes. In Tonk district’s Niwai area, intense rainfall lasted for about 20 minutes.
Strong winds were also reported in Jaipur since early morning. The rain and gusty winds caused inconvenience to devotees undertaking the Khatushyam foot pilgrimage along Sikar Road.
According to weather experts, cloud cover and changing weather conditions are likely to persist in districts such as Kota and Udaipur, apart from Jaipur.
Early summer likely due to weak western disturbances
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has warned that Rajasthan may experience an early onset of summer this year due to fewer western disturbances and below-normal rainfall.
Officials said heat conditions could intensify during Holi in the first week of March, with maximum daytime temperatures in some cities likely to reach 38 degrees Celsius or higher.
Residents along Jaipur–Sikar Road said cool winds have been blowing across the area since Sunday night, even as light rain was reported in parts of the region.
Day temperatures rise across state
Despite brief rain activity, daytime temperatures remain high across Rajasthan. Over the past 24 hours, most cities in Jaipur, Ajmer, Bikaner, Bharatpur and Jodhpur divisions recorded maximum temperatures between 29°C and 33°C due to clear skies and strong sunshine.
Barmer recorded the highest maximum temperature in the state at 34.6°C on Sunday. In Kota and Udaipur divisions, partial cloud cover slightly reduced the intensity of sunlight during the afternoon.
Temperatures remain above normal in several cities
Maximum temperatures on Sunday remained above normal in many parts of the state. Sri Ganganagar recorded temperatures 6.1°C above normal, while Pilani was 5.8°C above average.
Jaisalmer, Bikaner and Barmer recorded temperatures 3.8°C above normal, while Phalodi, Churu and Sikar were 3.4°C above average. Jaipur recorded temperatures 2°C above normal, with several other cities reporting temperatures 1°C to 2°C higher than average.
Clear weather forecast over next week
The IMD’s Jaipur centre has forecast mainly clear weather across most parts of Rajasthan over the next week. Regions including Jaipur, Ajmer, Jodhpur, Bikaner and Bharatpur are expected to see strong sunshine, with daytime temperatures around or above 30°C.
While light cloud cover may appear on February 23 and 24, no significant rainfall is expected. Weather officials said heat conditions are likely to strengthen gradually in the coming days, raising concerns about an early and intense summer season.
